The angiotensin receptor blocker valsartan significantly reduced TRPC6 and nuclear factor of activated T‑cells (NFAT) overexpression in diabetic rats. These results in vivo were confirmed by studies in vitro, which demonstrated that inhibition of TRPC6 ameliorated high glucose‑induced podocyte injury by decreasing NFAT mRNA levels.
